SEARCH
Package basicFiles.files

Class TabularFile

java.lang.Object
basicFiles.files.BaseFile
basicFiles.files.TabularFile
All Implemented Interfaces:
Iterable<List<String>>
public class TabularFile extends BaseFile implements Iterable<List<String>>
Class representing a tabular file (CSV, TSV, SSV, etc.). This class allows for reading and writing structured data as a list of string rows. It implements Iterable to facilitate row-by-row traversal.
Author:
Elias Kassas

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    TabularFile(File folder, String fileName)
    Specialized constructor for CSV files (comma-separated) within a directory.
    TabularFile(File folder, String fileName, char separator)
    Constructor for a generic tabular file within a specific directory.
    TabularFile(String fileName)
    Specialized constructor for CSV files in the current working directory.
    TabularFile(String fileName, char separator)
    Constructor for a generic tabular file with a custom separator.

  • Constructor Details

    • TabularFile

      public TabularFile(String fileName, char separator) throws IOException
      Constructor for a generic tabular file with a custom separator. Automatically loads the file into memory if it exists on disk.
      Parameters:
      fileName - The name of the file including its extension.
      separator - The character used as a field separator.
      Throws:
      IOException - If an error occurs during the initial read operation.

    • TabularFile

      public TabularFile(File folder, String fileName, char separator) throws IOException
      Constructor for a generic tabular file within a specific directory.
      Parameters:
      folder - The parent directory.
      fileName - The name of the file.
      separator - The field separator character.
      Throws:
      IOException - If an error occurs during reading.

    • TabularFile

      public TabularFile(File folder, String fileName) throws IOException
      Specialized constructor for CSV files (comma-separated) within a directory.
      Parameters:
      folder - The parent directory.
      fileName - The name of the file (extension .csv is handled by the base class).
      Throws:
      IOException - If an error occurs during reading.

    • TabularFile

      public TabularFile(String fileName) throws IOException
      Specialized constructor for CSV files in the current working directory.
      Parameters:
      fileName - The name of the file.
      Throws:
      IOException - If an error occurs during reading.

  • Method Details

    • getRow

      public List<String> getRow(int index)
      Retrieves the row located at the specified index.
      Parameters:
      index - The zero-based index of the row.
      Returns:
      A List of strings representing the row's fields.

    • setRow

      public List<String> setRow(int index, List<String> replacement) throws IOException
      Replaces the row at the given index and immediately synchronizes the change to disk.
      Parameters:
      index - The index of the row to replace.
      replacement - The new list of fields for the row.
      Returns:
      The previously stored row.
      Throws:
      IOException - If the write operation fails.

    • read

      public void read() throws IOException
      Loads tabular data from the physical file into the in-memory buffer. The file is parsed line by line according to the defined separator.
      Specified by:
      read in class BaseFile
      Throws:
      IOException - If the file cannot be read.

    • write

      public void write(Object... args) throws IOException
      Writes the current in-memory content to the physical file. If arguments are provided, they are added to the content before writing.
      Specified by:
      write in class BaseFile
      Parameters:
      args - Optional: A List<String> for a single row, a List<List<String>> for multiple rows, or a raw String line.
      Throws:
      IOException - If an error occurs during the write process.

    • getContent

      public List<List<String>> getContent()
      Returns a copy of the in-memory tabular data.
      Returns:
      A list of rows.

    • getContentString

      public List<String> getContentString()
      Returns the content as a list of raw strings joined by the separator.
      Returns:
      A list of formatted CSV/TSV lines.

    • clear

      public void clear() throws IOException
      Clears both the in-memory buffer and the physical file content.
      Throws:
      IOException - If the write operation fails.

    • iterator

      public Iterator<List<String>> iterator()
      Specified by:
      iterator in interface Iterable<List<String>>

    • deletePhysicalFile

      public boolean deletePhysicalFile()
      Description copied from class: BaseFile
      Physically deletes the file from the file system.
      Specified by:
      deletePhysicalFile in class BaseFile
      Returns:
      true if the file was successfully deleted; false otherwise.

    • toAlignedTableString

      public String toAlignedTableString()
      Generates a visually aligned, plain-text table representation of the content. Useful for console debugging or UI display.
      Returns:
      A string containing the aligned table.

    • removeRow

      public List<String> removeRow(int index) throws IOException
      Removes the row at the specified index and updates the disk.
      Parameters:
      index - The index of the row to remove.
      Returns:
      The removed row content.
      Throws:
      IOException - If the synchronization fails.

    • append

      public void append(Object... args) throws Exception
      Appending is currently not implemented for tabular files.
      Specified by:
      append in class BaseFile
      Parameters:
      args - Variable arguments used by subclasses for specialized appending.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - Always thrown.
      Exception - If an error occurs during the append operation.
